summary: Full backend + frontend smoke/integration tests for BetsTracker (FastAPI + MongoDB + React). Created pytest suite covering auth, strategies, bets, bonuses, export, authorization and WebSocket broadcast. All 24 backend tests passed. Executed end-to-end Playwright flow (login with migrated user -> home KPIs -> strategy detail -> add-bet -> strategies -> profile); all pages render, Live Sync indicator is green, no JS page errors.

minor.issue: allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False works for Bearer header flow, but server also reads access_token cookie in get_current_user; cookie auth is effectively unreachable cross-origin because no cookies are ever Set-Cookie'd on /api/auth/login or /api/auth/register. Login response returns token only. Either document Bearer-only, or set httpOnly cookie + switch CORS to explicit origin + allow_credentials=True.
No minimum-length / complexity validation on new_password; a 1-char password is accepted.
Does not preserve created_at (not mutated since body.model_dump() only has name/bankroll/type/archived, so created_at untouched — OK). No issue, just noted.
WebSocket has no ping/pong or keepalive; client-side reconnect exists but server relies on client sending text. Harmless but fragile under idle networks.

issues: Data e ora uses browser-default datetime-local picker instead of a styled component; acceptable per product decision (no shadcn) but inconsistent with the rest of the dark-mode UI.
Hardcoded vanity stats '2.758 Bet storicizzate / 44 Strategie' don't match the actual migrated dataset for the primary user (shows 3 strategies, 101 bets). Cosmetic only.

action_items: Optional: decide on cookie vs Bearer auth consistently. Current flow works via Bearer only — remove the cookie read in get_current_user OR implement Set-Cookie on login + proper CORS.
Optional: add minimum password length validation on register and change-password endpoints.
Optional: update seed-data marketing counters on Login page or make them dynamic.
No blocking issues — app is ready for user acceptance testing.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py get_current_user reads cookie `access_token` but nothing ever writes it — misleading. Either remove cookie path or add Set-Cookie on login.
CORS `allow_origins=['*']` with `allow_credentials=False` is fine for Bearer tokens but incompatible with cookie auth.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook recommends 5-attempt lockout).
compute_profit treats unknown result strings silently as 0 profit (same as pending/void). Could add validation on `result` field (Literal['pending','won','lost','void']).
WebSocket lacks server-side heartbeat; long-idle connections may silently drop without broadcast delivery.
server.py is ~460 lines and monolithic — OK for now, but splitting routers (auth/strategies/bets/bonuses) would improve maintainability if scope grows.

success_rate.frontend: 100% critical flows (login, home KPIs, strategy detail, add-bet, strategies list, profile all load; Live Sync WebSocket connected)

test_credentials: Primary: giulio.mantioni@hotmail.it / giulio.mantioni@hotmail.it (works). Migrated user shows 3 strategies / 101 bets (test_credentials.md states 1 strategy — doc drift, not a bug).
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral test users created during test runs via /api/auth/register with TEST_ prefixed resources; created resources are deleted within their tests. Primary user data untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest + websockets). Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Covers 24 test cases including WebSocket broadcast verification. Frontend data-testids: login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn, kpi-balance/pnl/roi/yield/winrate/bets, toggle-archived, go-strategies, strategy-card-{id}, home-add-bet. All critical UI flows verified; only cosmetic and minor hardening items remain.
rca of the issue: No critical issues found. Auth, CRUD, profit computation, cascade delete, export, and WebSocket broadcast all function per spec.
